content.text: How do I write a function in my Python script that reliably returns the current effective username on both Windows and Linux?
What happens if I run this on a system where the current process has been running under an elevated service account?
It will return the username associated with that elevated process ID. If you need a specific user, you should pass an UID/GID instead of relying on this function.
Got it, I'll integrate this into my main utility library.
